description:
Offered: every other year
This course provides an in-depth examination of poverty in the U.S. and its ramifications, using a sociological perspective. It analyzes critically the meaning of poverty, the distribution, the official measure and perspectives on the causes of poverty. In addition, the course explores the consequences of poverty, and discusses welfare reform and attitudes toward government assistance to the poor. Although the course focuses particular attention on the U.S., poverty in the developing world is also discussed.
Prerequisite(s): SOC 1100
Meets General Education Requirement(s): CV, DP
